name: Roasted Lemongrass Chicken Thighs (Mild)
description: The Woks of Life's one-pan roasted lemongrass chicken thighs — a fragrant Vietnamese-style marinade of lemongrass, shallot, garlic, fish sauce, oyster sauce, and lime, blitzed smooth and roasted with the chicken into a sticky pan sauce. Made MILD here (red pepper flakes omitted) for a guest-friendly dinner.
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons canola or vegetable oil (for the marinade)
1 teaspoon red pepper flakes — OMIT for a mild version
2 stalks fresh lemongrass, cut into pieces
8 chicken thighs, bone-in, skin-on (use ~6 for 4-5 servings)
2 tablespoons canola or vegetable oil (for cooking)
recipeInstructions: Combine the first 9 marinade ingredients (oil, fish sauce, oyster sauce, red pepper flakes [omit for mild], sugar, shallots, garlic, lemongrass, lime zest) in a food processor until smooth. Coat the chicken thighs, cover, and refrigerate 1-4 hours.
Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons oil in a 12-inch ovenproof skillet over medium-high. Place the chicken skin-side down and cook 4 minutes.
Flip the chicken, add the reserved marinade to the skillet, transfer to the oven, and roast 25-30 minutes until cooked through.
Brush the accumulated pan drippings over the chicken. Serve over jasmine rice with lime wedges (and Thai basil if you like).
author: The Woks of Life

ai.cliffNotes: Blitz a lemongrass-shallot-garlic-fish-sauce marinade, soak the thighs 1-4 hrs, then sear skin-side down and roast with the marinade into a sticky pan sauce. Mild when you skip the pepper flakes; spoon the drippings over jasmine rice.
keyTips: For a guest who's heat-averse, just omit the red pepper flakes — that's the only source of heat; the dish is all fragrance (lemongrass, lime, shallot) without it.
Don't discard the marinade — it goes into the pan with the chicken and roasts down into a sticky, crispy, delicious sauce.
Sear the thighs skin-side down first to seal in the juices (and render the skin) before they go in the oven, so the meat stays moist.
The pan drippings are the payoff — brush them back over the chicken and spoon them over steamed jasmine rice (or a noodle salad).
Marinate ahead (1-4 hrs) so the only weeknight work is the 4-minute sear plus a hands-off roast — active time stays low.
ai.source: Claude (prose-mined from The Woks of Life article)
